description:
You are playing the role of a backup-integrity engineer who has been handed three daily backup logs that are already present on the system.  
Your job is to isolate every line that indicates a checksum failure and produce two audit artefacts that other teams will later consume.

Filesystem layout that already exists when you start
1. /home/user/backups/2023-08-01/backup_20230801.log  
2. /home/user/backups/2023-08-02/backup_20230802.log  
3. /home/user/backups/2023-08-03/backup_20230803.log  

A new directory called /home/user/backup_audit must be created by you to keep the audit artefacts.

Regex requirement
Extract **only** the log lines that match this regular-expression in extended POSIX syntax:  
   `^20[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} ERROR .* checksum mismatch$`  
(Notice the single space between “ERROR” and the following filename; the match must stretch from start (`^`) to end (`$`) of the line.)

Artefact 1 – /home/user/backup_audit/extracted_errors.log
• Plain UTF-8 text.  
• Contains **only** the raw log lines that satisfy the regex.  
• Preserve original order; do not add or delete whitespace; one line per entry; no extra blank lines; terminate with a single newline (`\n`) at EOF.

Artefact 2 – /home/user/backup_audit/failed_checksums_summary.txt
• Plain UTF-8 text.  
• EXACT five lines, each terminated with `\n`:
  1. `Total checksum errors: N`     ← N must be the total number of matching lines found.  
  2. *blank line*                  ← just a newline, no spaces.  
  3. `2023-08-01: X`               ← X is the number of matches found on 2023-08-01  
  4. `2023-08-02: Y`               ← Y is the number of matches found on 2023-08-02  
  5. `2023-08-03: Z`               ← Z is the number of matches found on 2023-08-03  
• The ordering must stay exactly as shown above regardless of counts (even if a count is zero).

Step goals (high-level)
1. Inspect all three *.log files inside /home/user/backups/** recursively.  
2. Use one or more regex-driven commands to filter the checksum-error lines.  
3. Build /home/user/backup_audit; populate the two artefacts with strictly the formats described.  

Verification expectations
• /home/user/backup_audit must exist and be world-readable (at least `chmod 755`).  
• /home/user/backup_audit/extracted_errors.log must contain 3 lines, exactly the error lines present in the source logs, in chronological order, no blank lines before or after.  
• /home/user/backup_audit/failed_checksums_summary.txt must contain exactly the five described lines: first line with the grand total “3”; blank second line; then the per-day counts `1`, `2`, and `0` respectively.  

You will not need root privileges. Everything happens under /home/user.  
When you are finished, the automated test will read only the two output files and verify their exact byte-for-byte contents, as well as the presence and permissions of /home/user/backup_audit.  
Do **not** hard-code the answers; derive them programmatically from the logs.
truth:
Initial log files and their full contents (with trailing newline):

File: /home/user/backups/2023-08-01/backup_20230801.log
2023-08-01 00:59:59 INFO Starting backup
2023-08-01 01:15:42 ERROR db01.tar.gz checksum mismatch
2023-08-01 01:22:10 INFO db01.tar.gz uploaded
2023-08-01 01:30:00 INFO Backup finished

File: /home/user/backups/2023-08-02/backup_20230802.log
2023-08-02 00:59:58 INFO Starting backup
2023-08-02 01:12:02 ERROR web_assets.zip checksum mismatch
2023-08-02 01:12:03 WARN Retrying web_assets.zip upload
2023-08-02 01:44:22 ERROR db02.tar.gz checksum mismatch
2023-08-02 02:01:00 INFO Backup finished

File: /home/user/backups/2023-08-03/backup_20230803.log
2023-08-03 01:00:01 INFO Starting backup
2023-08-03 01:33:47 INFO No errors detected
2023-08-03 02:00:00 INFO Backup finished

Expected artefacts after correct completion:

1. /home/user/backup_audit/extracted_errors.log
2023-08-01 01:15:42 ERROR db01.tar.gz checksum mismatch
2023-08-02 01:12:02 ERROR web_assets.zip checksum mismatch
2023-08-02 01:44:22 ERROR db02.tar.gz checksum mismatch

(Each line ends with `\n`, no extra blank lines.)

2. /home/user/backup_audit/failed_checksums_summary.txt
Total checksum errors: 3

2023-08-01: 1
2023-08-02: 2
2023-08-03: 0

(Exactly five lines, each ending with `\n`.)

Required permissions to pass tests:
• /home/user/backup_audit : 755  
• /home/user/backup_audit/extracted_errors.log : 644  
• /home/user/backup_audit/failed_checksums_summary.txt : 644
